---
title: "Heatmap Widget Roadmap"
format: html
---

# HeatmapWidget Development Roadmap

This document tracks remaining development tasks for the Heatmap Widget. For documentation
of completed features and current architecture, see the [main developer documentation](index.qmd).

## Completed Work Summary

The following features are complete and documented in [index.qmd](index.qmd):

- **Widget skeleton**: EditorState pattern, registration, state/view/properties factories
- **State management**: `HeatmapState` with `PlotAlignmentState`, `RelativeTimeAxisState`, `VerticalAxisState` composition
- **Axis system**: `RelativeTimeAxisWidget` (X) and `VerticalAxisWidget` (Y) with bidirectional sync
- **Interaction**: Separated X/Y zoom, pan, double-click time navigation via shared `PlotInteractionHelpers`
- **Properties panel**: Alignment controls (`PlotAlignmentWidget`), axis range controls in collapsible sections
- **OpenGL lifecycle**: `SceneRenderer` initialization, background color, projection/view matrix management
- **State serialization**: Full round-trip JSON via `rfl::json`
- **Unit selection state** (Phase 1.1): `HeatmapStateData::unit_keys` with full add/remove/batch API and `unitsChanged()` signal
- **Unit selection UI** (Phase 1.2): `Feature_Tree_Widget` embedded in properties panel, filtered to `DigitalEventSeries`, with prefix-based group toggling
- **Rate estimation infrastructure** (Phase 1.3): `EventRateEstimation` library in `Plots/Common/EventRateEstimation/` providing `createUnitGatherContext()` / `createUnitGatherContexts()` for batched aligned gathering and `estimateRate()` / `estimateRates()` returning `RateEstimate` (paired `times[]` + `values[]`) with a `std::variant<BinningParams, GaussianKernelParams, CausalExponentialParams>`-based variation point. See [Rate Estimation Redesign](../Common/EventRateEstimation/rate_estimation_redesign.md) for the full API design.
- **Colormap infrastructure** (Phase 2): `CorePlotting::Colormaps` library in `CorePlotting/Colormaps/` providing `ColormapFunction`, `ColormapPreset` (Inferno, Viridis, Magma, Plasma, Coolwarm, Grayscale, Hot), LUT-based O(1) evaluation with linear interpolation, `mapValue()` / `mapValues()` / `mapMatrix()` utilities. Shared with SpectrogramWidget and any future external-feature coloring use cases.
- **Heatmap scene building** (Phase 1.4): `HeatmapMapper` in `CorePlotting/Mappers/` converting `HeatmapRowData` rows to `RenderableRectangleBatch` with Auto / Manual / Symmetric color range modes. `HeatmapOpenGLWidget::rebuildScene()` now gathers units via `createUnitGatherContexts()`, estimates rates via `estimateRates()`, applies scaling via `applyScaling()`, converts `RateEstimate` objects to `HeatmapRowData`, and uploads the scene.
- **Y-axis auto-fit** (Phase 1.5): `HeatmapWidget` auto-fits the vertical axis when the unit count changes. Y bounds are set to `[0, num_units]`, Y zoom is reset to 1.0, and Y pan is reset to 0.0 so all units are visible. The `VerticalAxisWidget` uses `identityAxis("Unit", 0)` for integer unit-index labels. Signal renamed from `trialCountChanged` to `unitCountChanged` for semantic clarity.
- **Scaling and normalization** (Phase 3): Five scaling modes (Firing Rate Hz, Z-Score, Normalized [0,1], Raw Count, Count/Trial). Uses shared `ScalingMode`/`applyScaling()` from `RateEstimate.hpp`. `HeatmapStateData` extended with `scaling` and `color_range` (Auto/Manual/Symmetric). Auto-switches to Coolwarm colormap for z-score and Symmetric color range. Properties panel has "Scaling & Color Range" collapsible section with `ScalingModeControls` widget and manual vmin/vmax spinboxes.
- **Rate estimation options** (Phase 5): `EstimationParams` variant (`std::variant<BinningParams, GaussianKernelParams, CausalExponentialParams>`) stored directly in `HeatmapStateData::estimation_params`. Lightweight `EstimationParams.hpp` header (no heavy transitive deps) safe to include from state headers. `RateEstimationControls` library provides composable Qt widgets: `EstimationMethodControls` (method combo + stacked per-method parameter pages) and `ScalingModeControls` (scaling mode combo). `HeatmapPropertiesWidget` uses both widgets. Renderer passes `estimationParams()` directly to `estimateRates()` — no intermediate conversion.

**Not yet implemented**: colorbar display (Phase 4), colormap selection UI (Phase 9), Gaussian/Causal method implementations (stubs in EstimationMethodControls show "Not yet implemented"), sorting, and all cross-widget linking described below.

---

## Phase 1: Core Rendering — Per-Unit Rate Matrix

**Status**: Phases 1.1–1.5 complete.

**Goal**: Produce a `units × time_bins` firing rate matrix and render it as a colored heatmap.

### 1.1 Unit Selection in State ✅

`HeatmapStateData` stores the selected units as a flat key list (serialized via `rfl::json`):

```cpp
struct HeatmapStateData {
    // ... existing fields ...
    std::vector<std::string> unit_keys;  ///< Selected DigitalEventSeries keys
};
```

`HeatmapState` exposes a full management API:

```cpp
void addUnit(std::string const & key);
void removeUnit(std::string const & key);
void addUnits(std::vector<std::string> const & keys);    // batch
void removeUnits(std::vector<std::string> const & keys); // batch
[[nodiscard]] bool hasUnit(std::string const & key) const;
[[nodiscard]] std::vector<std::string> const & unitKeys() const;
```

All mutating methods emit `unitsChanged()` (for UI sync) and `stateChanged()` (for scene
rebuild). Addition is idempotent — duplicate keys are silently ignored.

Note: `HeatmapUnitConfig` (with a separate `display_label`) was simplified to a plain
`std::string` key list. Labels are derived from the key at render time. A dedicated config
struct can be introduced if per-unit labels or colors are needed in a future phase.

| Signal | Trigger |
|--------|---------|
| `unitsChanged()` | Any change to the unit list (add, remove, or batch) |
| `stateChanged()` (inherited) | Combined with `unitsChanged()` to trigger scene rebuild |

### 1.2 Unit Selection UI ✅

`HeatmapPropertiesWidget` now embeds a `Feature_Tree_Widget` (the same component used by
`DataViewer_Widget`) filtered to `DM_DataType::DigitalEvent` only:

- **Available keys** are shown in a tree, auto-grouped by prefix pattern
  (e.g. `unit_1`, `unit_2`, `unit_3` appear under a `unit` group node)
- **Checkbox per item** adds/removes that key from `HeatmapState::unit_keys`
- **Group checkbox** adds/removes all keys with the same prefix in a single click
- **Live updates** — the tree re-populates automatically when DataManager changes
  (via the DataManager observer; the observer is properly unregistered on widget destruction)
- **State sync** — on construction, existing `unit_keys` from serialized state are
  reflected in the tree checkboxes

This replaces the combo box + Add/Remove/Add-All button design described in the original
roadmap; the tree gives equivalent functionality with less UI surface and more visual
structure when many units are present.

### 1.3 Rate Estimation ✅

The shared `EventRateEstimation` library (`Plots/Common/EventRateEstimation/`) provides all
gathering and rate estimation infrastructure needed by both this widget and
[PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd).

The library is being redesigned per the
[Rate Estimation Redesign](../Common/EventRateEstimation/rate_estimation_redesign.md). Key
changes: `RateProfile` → `RateEstimate` (paired `times[]` + `values[]`),
`HeatmapScaling`/`normalizeRateProfiles()` → shared `ScalingMode`/`applyScaling()`,
new `estimateRateWithTrials()` for uncertainty quantification.

**Gathering API** (unchanged):

```cpp
// Single unit — returns std::nullopt if gather fails or TimeFrame is missing
std::optional<UnitGatherContext> createUnitGatherContext(
    std::shared_ptr<DataManager> const & dm,
    std::string const & event_key,
    PlotAlignmentData const & alignment_data);

// Batch — skips failing keys, preserves order of successes
std::vector<UnitGatherContext> createUnitGatherContexts(
    std::shared_ptr<DataManager> const & dm,
    std::vector<std::string> const & event_keys,
    PlotAlignmentData const & alignment_data);
```

`UnitGatherContext` bundles the `GatherResult<DigitalEventSeries>`, source `TimeFrame`, and
key so callers do not need to re-fetch the `TimeFrame` at estimation time.

**Rate estimation API** (after redesign):

```cpp
// Single unit — returns paired times[] + values[]
RateEstimate estimateRate(
    GatherResult<DigitalEventSeries> const & gathered,
    TimeFrame const * time_frame,
    double window_size,
    EstimationParams const & params = BinningParams{});

// Multi-unit (heatmap use case)
std::vector<RateEstimate> estimateRates(
    std::vector<UnitGatherContext> const & units,
    double window_size,
    EstimationParams const & params = BinningParams{});
```

`RateEstimate` stores paired `times[]` + `values[]` with `num_trials` and `metadata.sample_spacing`.
The output is method-agnostic — binning, kernel smoothing, and adaptive methods all produce
the same output type.

**Variation point**: `EstimationParams` is a `std::variant<BinningParams, GaussianKernelParams, ...>`.
New methods are added by defining a new `*Params` struct and appending it to the variant.

### 1.4 Heatmap Scene Building ✅

`HeatmapOpenGLWidget::rebuildScene()` now implements the full data pipeline:

1. Guard: returns early if no units are selected or alignment state is missing
2. Calls `createUnitGatherContexts()` to gather trial-aligned `DigitalEventSeries` for all selected units
3. Calls `estimateRates()` to get `RateEstimate` objects for each unit
4. Applies scaling via `applyScaling()` in-place
5. Converts `RateEstimate` objects to `HeatmapRowData` (using `times[]` bin centers and `metadata.sample_spacing` to reconstruct left edges)
6. Calls `HeatmapMapper::buildScene()` with the appropriate colormap and color range
7. Uploads the scene via `_scene_renderer.uploadScene()` and emits `unitCountChanged`

The new `HeatmapMapper` lives in `CorePlotting/Mappers/` and is decoupled from `EventRateEstimation`
(accepts generic `HeatmapRowData` rather than `RateProfile`) so it can be reused by SpectrogramWidget
without introducing an upward dependency.

### 1.5 Y-Axis Auto-fit ✅

When `unitCountChanged` is emitted from `HeatmapOpenGLWidget::rebuildScene()`, `HeatmapWidget`
auto-fits the vertical axis:

- Sets Y bounds to `[0, num_units]` via `HeatmapState::setYBounds()`
- Resets Y zoom to 1.0 and Y pan to 0.0 so all units fill the viewport
- Syncs `VerticalAxisState` range controls
- The `VerticalAxisWidget` uses `CorePlotting::identityAxis("Unit", 0)` for integer unit-index
  labels (0, 1, 2, …). The identity mapping is correct because `HeatmapMapper` places rows
  at world Y ∈ [0, num_units] (row *i* spans Y = [i, i+1]).

Note: The signal was renamed from `trialCountChanged` to `unitCountChanged` and the cached
count from `_trial_count` to `_unit_count` to accurately reflect that heatmap rows represent
neural units, not trials.

---

## Phase 2: Colormap Infrastructure

**Status**: Complete. Implemented as `CorePlotting::Colormaps` in `src/CorePlotting/Colormaps/`.

**Goal**: Provide a reusable colormap system for mapping scalar values to RGBA colors.

### 2.1 Colormap Types ✅

Implemented in `src/CorePlotting/Colormaps/Colormap.hpp`:

```cpp
namespace CorePlotting::Colormaps {

using ColormapFunction = std::function<glm::vec4(float t)>;
using ColormapLUT = std::array<glm::vec3, 256>;

enum class ColormapPreset {
    Inferno, Viridis, Magma, Plasma,  // perceptually uniform
    Coolwarm,                          // diverging blue-white-red
    Grayscale, Hot                     // utility
};

ColormapFunction getColormap(ColormapPreset preset);
ColormapFunction fromLUT(ColormapLUT const & lut);
glm::vec4 mapValue(ColormapFunction const &, float value, float vmin, float vmax);
std::vector<glm::vec4> mapValues(ColormapFunction const &, std::span<double const>, float vmin, float vmax);
std::vector<glm::vec4> mapMatrix(ColormapFunction const &, std::span<double const>, size_t rows, size_t cols, float vmin, float vmax);
ColormapLUT const & getLUT(ColormapPreset preset);  // for colorbar preview
std::string presetName(ColormapPreset preset);
std::vector<ColormapPreset> allPresets();

}  // namespace CorePlotting::Colormaps
```

### 2.2 Location ✅

Colormap utilities are in `src/CorePlotting/Colormaps/`:

```
CoreMatching/Colormaps/
├── Colormap.hpp       # Public API — ColormapFunction, presets, mapValue/mapValues/mapMatrix
├── Colormap.cpp       # LUT-based evaluation with linear interpolation
└── ColormapLUTs.hpp   # LUT data for all presets (17-point control tables, elaborated to 256 entries)
```

The perceptually uniform colormaps (Inferno, Viridis, Magma, Plasma) use 17-point control
tables interpolated to 256 entries. Grayscale, Hot, and Coolwarm are generated
algorithmically. All LUTs are lazy-initialized as static singletons.

### 2.3 Shared Consumers

| Widget | Usage |
|--------|-------|
| **HeatmapWidget** | Rate matrix → colored heatmap cells |
| **[SpectrogramWidget](../SpectrogramWidget/index.qmd)** | Power spectrum matrix → colored spectrogram cells |
| **[EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/index.qmd)** | External feature coloring ([EventPlot Roadmap](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 5) |

### 2.4 Default Colormap

The HeatmapWidget should default to **Inferno**, which is perceptually uniform and accessible
for color vision deficiency. When z-score scaling is active (see [Phase 3](#phase-3-scaling-and-normalization)),
automatically suggest **Coolwarm** (diverging around zero).

---

## Phase 3: Scaling and Normalization

**Status**: Complete (initial implementation). Will be migrated to shared `ScalingMode`/`applyScaling()` from
the [Rate Estimation Redesign](../Common/EventRateEstimation/rate_estimation_redesign.md).

**Goal**: Support multiple Y-value (color intensity) representations for the heatmap cells.

### 3.1 Scaling Mode ✅ → Migration Pending

Currently uses a bespoke `HeatmapScaling` enum in `EventRateEstimation/RateScaling.hpp`.
This will be replaced by the shared `ScalingMode` enum from `RateEstimate.hpp`:

```cpp
enum class ScalingMode {
    FiringRateHz,
    ZScore,
    Normalized01,
    RawCount,
    CountPerTrial,
};
```

The scaling is applied in-place via `applyScaling(RateEstimate &, ScalingMode, time_units_per_second)`
rather than producing a separate `NormalizedRow` type. See
[Rate Estimation Redesign — Normalization](../Common/EventRateEstimation/rate_estimation_redesign.md#normalization-as-composable-transforms).

Helper functions `scalingLabel()`, `scalingAxisLabel()`, and `allScalingModes()` remain
available for UI population.

### 3.2 State Extension ✅ → Migration Pending

`HeatmapStateData` includes the scaling mode and color range configuration. After migration,
`HeatmapScaling` references will be replaced with `ScalingMode`:

```cpp
struct HeatmapColorRangeConfig {
    enum class Mode { Auto, Manual, Symmetric };
    Mode mode = Mode::Auto;
    double vmin = 0.0;
    double vmax = 1.0;
};

struct HeatmapStateData {
    // ... existing fields ...
    WhiskerToolbox::Plots::ScalingMode scaling =
            WhiskerToolbox::Plots::ScalingMode::FiringRateHz;
    HeatmapColorRangeConfig color_range;
};
```

`HeatmapState` exposes `scaling()`, `setScaling()`, `colorRange()`, `setColorRangeMode()`,
`setColorRangeBounds()`, and `setColorRange()`. Setting z-score scaling auto-switches the
color range to `Symmetric`; leaving z-score reverts to `Auto`.

| Signal | Trigger |
|--------|---------|
| `scalingChanged()` | Scaling mode changed |
| `colorRangeChanged()` | Color range mode or bounds changed |

### 3.3 Implementation ✅ → Migration Pending

Currently implemented via `normalizeRateProfiles()`, which takes raw `RateProfile` objects
and a `HeatmapScaling` mode and returns `NormalizedRow` objects. After the redesign migration:

- `normalizeRateProfiles()` → `applyScaling(RateEstimate &, ScalingMode, time_units_per_second)`
  applied in-place to each `RateEstimate`
- `NormalizedRow` is eliminated — the `RateEstimate` itself is the ready-to-render output
- The `RateProfile` → `NormalizedRow` → `HeatmapRowData` conversion chain simplifies to
  `RateEstimate` → `HeatmapRowData` (single step, using `times[]` centers and `metadata.sample_spacing`)

See [Rate Estimation Redesign — Bridging to Rendering](../Common/EventRateEstimation/rate_estimation_redesign.md#bridging-to-rendering).

The colormap automatically switches to **Coolwarm** for z-score scaling and **Inferno** for
all other modes. The `HeatmapColorRangeConfig` from state is mapped to
`CorePlotting::HeatmapColorRange` for the mapper.

### 3.4 Color Range Controls ✅

Implemented via the `HeatmapColorRangeConfig` struct (in `HeatmapState.hpp`) with
three modes:

- **Auto**: Range from data min/max (handled by `HeatmapMapper::computeAutoRange`)
- **Manual**: User-specified `vmin`/`vmax` (spinboxes visible only in Manual mode)
- **Symmetric**: Range symmetric around zero (useful for z-score)

The `HeatmapColorRangeConfig` is serialized alongside other state via `rfl::json`.

Note: Percentile clipping for Auto mode is deferred to a future enhancement.

### 3.5 Properties Panel ✅

A "Scaling & Color Range" collapsible `Section` is added to `HeatmapPropertiesWidget`:

- **Scaling mode** combo box (all five modes, populated from `allScalingModes()`)
- **Color range mode** combo box (Auto / Manual / Symmetric)
- **Min / Max spinboxes** (visible only when Manual mode is selected)

The UI auto-syncs bidirectionally with `HeatmapState` via `scalingChanged()` and
`colorRangeChanged()` signals with signal blockers to prevent feedback loops.

### 3.6 Unit Tests ✅ → Update Pending

Comprehensive tests for `normalizeRateProfiles()` exist in
`tests/WhiskerToolbox/Plots/Common/EventRateEstimation.test.cpp`. These will need to be
updated after the redesign migration to test `applyScaling()` and the composable
normalization primitives (`toFiringRateHz`, `zScoreNormalize`, etc.) instead.

Existing coverage:

- All five scaling modes with synthetic data
- Edge cases (zero trials, constant signal, empty input)
- Multiple units with per-unit independence
- Helper function tests (scalingLabel, allScalingModes)

---

## Phase 4: Colorbar Display

**Status**: Not started. Phases 2 and 3 are complete; this phase can proceed.

**Goal**: Render a color legend alongside the heatmap showing the value-to-color mapping.

### 4.1 ColorbarWidget

Create a lightweight `QWidget` that renders a vertical color gradient with tick labels:

```
CorePlotting/Widgets/
├── ColorbarWidget.hpp
└── ColorbarWidget.cpp
```

The `ColorbarWidget` needs:

- A `ColormapFunction` to render the gradient
- A value range (`vmin`, `vmax`) for tick label computation
- An axis label (e.g., "Firing Rate (Hz)", "Z-Score")
- Optional tick formatting (integer, decimal, scientific notation)

### 4.2 Integration with HeatmapWidget

Place the colorbar to the right of the OpenGL canvas in the `HeatmapWidget` layout:

```
┌──────────────────────────────────┬────┐
│                                  │    │
│       HeatmapOpenGLWidget        │ CB │
│                                  │    │
├──────────────────────────────────┴────┤
│         RelativeTimeAxisWidget        │
└───────────────────────────────────────┘
```

The colorbar updates when the colormap, scaling mode, or value range changes.

### 4.3 Shared Consumer

The `ColorbarWidget` should be reusable by [SpectrogramWidget](../SpectrogramWidget/index.qmd),
which also needs a color legend for its power spectrum display.

---

## Phase 5: Shared Rate Estimation Options ✅

**Status**: Complete.
Closes related to [PSTH Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 2.

**Goal**: Share estimation method selection and parameters between HeatmapWidget
and [PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd) via the shared `EstimationParams` variant
from the [Rate Estimation Redesign](../Common/EventRateEstimation/rate_estimation_redesign.md).

### 5.1 State Extension ✅

`HeatmapStateData` stores the full `EstimationParams` variant directly:

```cpp
struct HeatmapStateData {
    // ... existing fields ...
    WhiskerToolbox::Plots::EstimationParams estimation_params;  ///< Default: BinningParams{}
};
```

All method-specific parameters are preserved inside the variant regardless of which method
is active. Storing the variant (rather than individual scalar fields) means no runtime
reconstruction is needed — `HeatmapOpenGLWidget::rebuildScene()` passes
`_state->estimationParams()` directly to `estimateRates()`.

`HeatmapState` exposes:

```cpp
[[nodiscard]] WhiskerToolbox::Plots::EstimationParams const & estimationParams() const;
void setEstimationParams(WhiskerToolbox::Plots::EstimationParams const & params);
```

`setEstimationParams()` uses `!=` comparison (backed by defaulted `operator==` on each
param struct) to avoid spurious redraws. On change it emits `estimationParamsChanged()` and
`stateChanged()`.

**Header design**: `EstimationParams.hpp` (`Plots/Common/EventRateEstimation/EstimationParams.hpp`)
is a standalone lightweight header that depends only on `<variant>`. This makes it safe to
include from state headers without pulling in the full `EventRateEstimation.hpp` chain
(DataManager, GatherResult, etc.).

### 5.2 Estimation Methods ✅

All methods are defined in `EstimationParams.hpp`:

```cpp
struct BinningParams {
    double bin_size = 10.0; ///< Bin width (same units as window_size)
    [[nodiscard]] bool operator==(BinningParams const &) const = default;
};

struct GaussianKernelParams {
    double sigma = 20.0;    ///< Kernel bandwidth
    double eval_step = 1.0; ///< Evaluation point spacing
    [[nodiscard]] bool operator==(GaussianKernelParams const &) const = default;
};

struct CausalExponentialParams {
    double tau = 50.0;      ///< Exponential time constant
    double eval_step = 1.0; ///< Evaluation point spacing
    [[nodiscard]] bool operator==(CausalExponentialParams const &) const = default;
};

using EstimationParams = std::variant<BinningParams, GaussianKernelParams, CausalExponentialParams>;
```

| Variant | Implemented | Description |
|---------|-------------|-------------|
| `BinningParams` | ✅ | Simple histogram binning (default) |
| `GaussianKernelParams` | Stub | Convolution with Gaussian kernel |
| `CausalExponentialParams` | Stub | Causal exponential filter |

For the heatmap, estimation is applied independently per unit. The output is always
`RateEstimate` with paired `times[]` + `values[]` regardless of method.

### 5.3 RateEstimationControls Library ✅

The shared `RateEstimationControls` static library (`Plots/Common/RateEstimationControls/`)
provides two composable `QWidget` subclasses that can be embedded in any properties panel:

**`EstimationMethodControls`** — method selection + per-method parameters:

```
┌────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Method: [Histogram Binning ▼]      │
│ ┌──────────────────────────────┐   │
│ │  Bin Size: [10.0 ms ▲▼]     │   │  ← stacked page for BinningParams
│ └──────────────────────────────┘   │
└────────────────────────────────────┘
```

- Combo box with all three methods (`Histogram Binning`, `Gaussian Kernel (stub)`,
  `Causal Exponential (stub)`)
- `QStackedWidget` — one page per method with method-specific spinboxes
- Stub pages show a "Not yet implemented" label with controls disabled
- Emits `paramsChanged(EstimationParams)` whenever any parameter changes
- `setParams(EstimationParams const &)` updates the combo and the active page (with
  `_updating_ui` anti-recursion guard)

**`ScalingModeControls`** — scaling mode selection:

- Combo box auto-populated from `allScalingModes()`
- Optional label text via constructor parameter
- Emits `scalingModeChanged(ScalingMode)`
- `setScalingMode(ScalingMode)` with `_updating_ui` guard

Both widgets follow the `GlyphStyleControls` composable-widget pattern used elsewhere in
the project.

### 5.4 Properties Panel Integration ✅

`HeatmapPropertiesWidget` now uses both composable controls inside its "Scaling & Estimation"
section:

```cpp
// Setup (in _setupScalingSection)
_estimation_controls = new EstimationMethodControls(this);
_estimation_controls->setParams(_state->estimationParams());
connect(_estimation_controls, &EstimationMethodControls::paramsChanged,
        _state, &HeatmapState::setEstimationParams);

_scaling_controls = new ScalingModeControls("Scaling", this);
_scaling_controls->setScalingMode(_state->scaling());
connect(_scaling_controls, &ScalingModeControls::scalingModeChanged,
        _state, &HeatmapState::setScaling);

// Sync from state
void _syncScalingFromState() {
    _estimation_controls->setParams(_state->estimationParams());
    _scaling_controls->setScalingMode(_state->scaling());
}
```

No `BinningParams` construction or `std::visit` extraction is needed at the widget layer —
the `EstimationParams` value flows unchanged from UI → state → renderer.

---

## Phase 6: Row Sorting

**Status**: Not started. Phase 1 is now complete; this phase can proceed.

**Goal**: Allow users to reorder heatmap rows by various criteria for pattern discovery.

### 6.1 Sorting Modes

```cpp
enum class HeatmapSortMode {
    Manual,         ///< User-defined order (default — order of addition)
    TimeToPeak,     ///< Sort by latency of peak rate relative to t=0 (ascending)
    PeakRate,       ///< Sort by maximum rate value (descending)
    MeanRate,       ///< Sort by mean rate across all bins (descending)
    Alphabetical    ///< Sort by unit label / event key (ascending)
};
```

### 6.2 Time-to-Peak Sorting

The primary sorting mode. For each unit row:

1. Find the bin with the maximum rate value
2. The bin's center time relative to t = 0 is the "time to peak"
3. Sort units by time to peak in ascending order

This produces the characteristic "diagonal stripe" pattern seen in neuroscience publications,
revealing temporal sequences in population activity.

### 6.3 State Extension

```cpp
struct HeatmapStateData {
    // ... existing fields ...
    HeatmapSortMode sort_mode = HeatmapSortMode::Manual;
    bool sort_ascending = true;
};
```

### 6.4 Implementation

Sorting is applied after rate matrix computation and before colormap mapping:

```cpp
auto sort_indices = computeSortOrder(rate_matrix, sort_mode, sort_ascending);
auto sorted_matrix = reorderRows(rate_matrix, sort_indices);
auto sorted_units = reorderVector(units, sort_indices);
```

The sorted order should also be reflected in the Y-axis labels.

### 6.5 Properties Panel

Add a "Sorting" combo box to the properties panel with the available modes. Include an
ascending/descending toggle button.

---

## Phase 7: Cross-Widget Linking

**Status**: Design complete. Depends on [Phase 1](#phase-1-core-rendering--per-unit-rate-matrix).
Related to [EventPlot Roadmap](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 6 and
[PSTH Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 4.

**Goal**: Clicking a heatmap row updates [PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd) and
[EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/index.qmd) to show the selected unit's data.

### 7.1 SelectionContext Integration

Following the existing `SelectionContext` pattern used by DataInspector widgets:

```cpp
// When user clicks a heatmap row:
void HeatmapOpenGLWidget::mouseReleaseEvent(QMouseEvent * event) {
    if (!_is_panning) {
        int unit_index = worldToUnitIndex(screenToWorld(event->pos()));
        auto const & unit_config = _state->units().at(unit_index);
        // Emit via SelectionContext:
        SelectionContext::instance().setSelectedData(
            unit_config.event_key, SelectionSource::HeatmapWidget);
    }
}
```

### 7.2 Receiving Widgets

In [PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd) and [EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/index.qmd)
(if not pinned):

```cpp
void PSTHPropertiesWidget::_onSelectionChanged(SelectionSource const & source) {
    if (source.type != SelectionSourceType::HeatmapWidget) return;
    if (_state->isPinned()) return;
    auto key = source.data_key;
    auto type = _data_manager->getDataType(key);
    if (type == DataType::DigitalEventSeries) {
        _state->clearPlotEvents();
        _state->addPlotEvent(key, PSTHEventOptions{.event_key = key});
        // Scene rebuilds automatically via stateChanged()
    }
}
```

### 7.3 Row Click → Unit Selection Flow

The primary cross-widget workflow:

```
HeatmapWidget row click
        ↓
SelectionContext::setSelectedData("unit_42", source)
        ↓
PSTHWidget (if not pinned) receives selectionChanged
        ↓
Clears existing events, adds "unit_42" → rebuilds histogram
        ↓
EventPlotWidget (if not pinned) also receives selectionChanged
        ↓
Updates raster plot for "unit_42"
```

This enables rapid exploration of neural data: click through units in the heatmap while
the PSTH and raster plot update in sync. See [EventPlot Roadmap](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd)
Phase 6 and [PSTH Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 4 for the complementary
receiver implementations.

### 7.4 Visual Row Highlighting

When a row is selected (either by click or via incoming `SelectionContext`), highlight
the selected row with a border or background overlay to provide visual feedback.

### 7.5 Pin Button

Add a `pinned` field to `HeatmapStateData` and a pin/unpin toggle button to the properties
panel toolbar. When pinned, the widget ignores `SelectionContext` changes, preserving its
current view. The pinned state is serialized for workspace restoration.

---

## Phase 8: Tooltip with Stacked PSTH + Raster

**Status**: Design exploration. Depends on [Phase 1](#phase-1-core-rendering--per-unit-rate-matrix)
and [Phase 7](#phase-7-cross-widget-linking).

**Goal**: When hovering over a heatmap row, display a tooltip containing a miniature stacked
PSTH histogram and raster plot for that unit.

### 8.1 Concept

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   Heatmap                           │
│  unit_A  ██████████████████████████████████████████  │
│  unit_B  ██████████████████████████████████████████  │
│  unit_C  ████████▓▓▓▓████████████████████████████  ← hover
│  unit_D  ██████████████████████████████████████████  │
│                                                     │
│          ┌─────────────────────┐                    │
│          │  unit_C PSTH        │                    │
│          │  ▄▄ ██ ▄▄ ..       │                    │
│          │─────────────────────│                    │
│          │  unit_C Raster      │                    │
│          │  | || |  || |       │                    │
│          │  ||  | | |  ||      │                    │
│          └─────────────────────┘                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 8.2 Lightweight Rendering

The tooltip must render quickly without creating full widget instances. Two approaches:

**Option A: Pre-rendered QPixmap Cache**

When the scene rebuilds, pre-render PSTH and raster thumbnails for each unit into `QPixmap`
objects. The tooltip simply displays the cached pixmap. This has memory cost proportional to
the number of units but provides instant tooltips.

**Option B: On-Demand QPainter Rendering**

When the hover event fires, compute the PSTH bins and raster tick positions for the hovered
unit using the cached `GatherResult` data, and render directly with `QPainter` into a tooltip
widget. This avoids the memory cost but requires fast rendering.

**Recommendation**: Option B with the cached `GatherResult` data from the scene rebuild.
The rate computation uses the shared `estimateRate()` from the `EventRateEstimation` library
(the same function used by the heatmap itself), and `QPainter` rendering of a simple
histogram + tick marks is lightweight.

### 8.3 Tooltip Content

The tooltip should show:

1. **Header**: Unit label / event key
2. **PSTH subplot**: Bar or line histogram of the unit's rate profile (same data as the heatmap row)
3. **Raster subplot**: Tick marks for each trial's events (using the unit's `GatherResult`)
4. **Optional statistics**: Peak rate, time to peak, mean rate

### 8.4 Implementation Approach

- Use a custom `QWidget` subclass as the tooltip (not `QToolTip::showText()`)
- Position the tooltip near the cursor using `QCursor::pos()`
- Show/hide with a short delay timer (similar to `EventPlotOpenGLWidget`'s tooltip timer)
- Reuse the shared rate estimation function for the PSTH portion
- Reuse `RasterMapper` logic for the raster portion (a simplified version without full scene building)

This is related to [PSTH Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 6 (Embeddable PSTH for Tooltips),
which proposes decoupling the PSTH computation and rendering from the full widget machinery.

---

## Phase 9: Colormap Selection UI

**Status**: Not started. Phase 2 (colormap infrastructure) is now complete; this phase can proceed.

**Goal**: Allow users to select and preview colormaps from the properties panel.

### 9.1 Colormap Combo Box

Add a combo box to `HeatmapPropertiesWidget` that lists available `ColormapPreset` values.
Each entry should include a small colored gradient preview (rendered as a `QIcon` using the
colormap's 256-entry LUT).

### 9.2 State Extension

```cpp
struct HeatmapStateData {
    // ... existing fields ...
    ColormapPreset colormap = ColormapPreset::Inferno;
};
```

### 9.3 Auto-Suggestion

When the user switches to z-score scaling, automatically suggest the `Coolwarm` diverging
colormap (show a prompt or auto-switch with undo capability).

---

## Phase 10: PNG Export

**Status**: Not started. Qt provides the underlying infrastructure via `QOpenGLWidget::grabFramebuffer()`.

**Goal**: Export the current heatmap view as a publication-quality PNG image.

### 10.1 Basic Grab

The simplest approach uses Qt's built-in framebuffer grab:

```cpp
void HeatmapWidget::exportPNG(QString const & path) {
    QImage image = _opengl_widget->grabFramebuffer();
    image.save(path, "PNG");
}
```

This captures exactly what is displayed, including the current zoom/pan state.

### 10.2 High-Resolution Export

For publication quality, render at a higher resolution than screen:

```cpp
void HeatmapWidget::exportPNG(QString const & path, int scale_factor) {
    // Resize OpenGL widget to scaled dimensions
    // Render at high resolution
    // Grab framebuffer
    // Restore original size
}
```

### 10.3 Composite Export with Axes and Colorbar

For a complete figure, composite the OpenGL framebuffer with axis labels and colorbar:

1. Render the heatmap at target resolution
2. Render axes using `QPainter` at matching resolution
3. Render colorbar using `QPainter`
4. Composite into a single `QImage`
5. Save as PNG

### 10.4 UI Integration

Add an "Export PNG..." action to:

- The properties panel (button or menu)
- A right-click context menu on the plot

The dialog should offer:

- File path selection
- Resolution scale factor (1x, 2x, 4x)
- Include axes checkbox
- Include colorbar checkbox

### 10.5 SVG Export (Future)

The [PSTH Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) Phase 5 proposes shared SVG export infrastructure
for all plot widgets using `SceneRenderer`. The HeatmapWidget should adopt this when available,
as SVG provides resolution-independent output for publications.

---

## Phase 11: AnalogTimeSeries Heatmap Variant (Future)

**Status**: Early conceptual design. Separate from the DigitalEventSeries heatmap.

**Goal**: A sibling widget that creates heatmaps from `AnalogTimeSeries` data rather than
discrete events. This enables visualization of continuous signals like ΔF/F calcium imaging
traces across multiple cells, aligned to events.

### 11.1 Concept

Where the current HeatmapWidget computes rate from discrete events (analogous to stacking
[PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd) rows), the AnalogTimeSeries variant would display raw
continuous signals (analogous to stacking [LinePlotWidget](../LinePlotWidget/index.qmd) rows).

| Feature | Event Heatmap (current) | Analog Heatmap (future) |
|---------|------------------------|------------------------|
| Data source | `DigitalEventSeries` | `AnalogTimeSeries` |
| Per-unit computation | Binning + rate estimation | Direct value sampling / interpolation |
| Analogous single-unit view | [PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd) | [LinePlotWidget](../LinePlotWidget/index.qmd) |
| Typical use case | Population spike rate | ΔF/F, LFP, EMG across units |
| Normalization | Firing rate, z-score | Z-score, ΔF/F₀, % change |

### 11.2 Shared Infrastructure

Despite the different data sources, significant infrastructure is shared:

- **Colormap system** ([Phase 2](#phase-2-colormap-infrastructure))
- **Colorbar widget** ([Phase 4](#phase-4-colorbar-display))
- **Sorting modes** ([Phase 6](#phase-6-row-sorting)) — time-to-peak, peak value, etc.
- **Cross-widget linking** ([Phase 7](#phase-7-cross-widget-linking)) — click row → update linked widgets
- **PNG/SVG export** ([Phase 10](#phase-10-png-export))
- **Scaling/normalization** ([Phase 3](#phase-3-scaling-and-normalization)) — z-score, 0-1 normalization

### 11.3 Different Infrastructure

Key differences that may warrant a separate widget rather than a mode switch:

- **Data gathering**: `GatherResult<AnalogTimeSeries>` instead of `GatherResult<DigitalEventSeries>`
- **Value computation**: Direct sampling/interpolation instead of binning + rate estimation
- **Alignment**: May align to `DigitalIntervalSeries` only (baseline period for ΔF/F₀ normalization)
- **Time resolution**: Native sampling rate rather than binned; may need downsampling for display
- **Tooltip**: Would show a line trace rather than a PSTH histogram

### 11.4 Recommendation

Implement as a **separate widget** (`AnalogHeatmapWidget`) that reuses the colormap,
colorbar, sorting, linking, and export infrastructure. The rendering and data gathering
paths are sufficiently different that forcing them into a single widget would add unwanted
complexity. The two widgets can coexist in the Plot menu, clearly labeled:

- **Plot → Heatmap Plot (Events)** — current widget
- **Plot → Heatmap Plot (Analog)** — future widget

---

## Implementation Priority

### Next Up (Core Functionality)

1. **[Phase 3](#phase-3-scaling-and-normalization)**: Scaling/normalization modes (FiringRate, ZScore, Normalized01, RawCount, CountPerTrial)
2. **[Phase 4](#phase-4-colorbar-display)**: Colorbar display (reuse `CorePlotting::Colormaps::getLUT()` for gradient preview)

### Recently Completed

- **Phase 1.5**: Y-axis auto-fit — `unitCountChanged` signal, identity axis with "Unit" title, auto-reset zoom/pan
- **Phase 1.4**: Heatmap scene building — full `rebuildScene()` pipeline live
- **Phase 2**: Colormap infrastructure — `CorePlotting::Colormaps` in `src/CorePlotting/Colormaps/`

### Following (Polish and Features)

4. **[Phase 4](#phase-4-colorbar-display)**: Colorbar display
5. **[Phase 5](#phase-5-shared-rate-estimation-options)**: Shared rate estimation options (with PSTHWidget)
6. **[Phase 6](#phase-6-row-sorting)**: Row sorting (time-to-peak first)
7. **[Phase 9](#phase-9-colormap-selection-ui)**: Colormap selection UI

### Integration

8. **[Phase 7](#phase-7-cross-widget-linking)**: Cross-widget linking
9. **[Phase 8](#phase-8-tooltip-with-stacked-psth--raster)**: Tooltip with stacked PSTH + raster

### Export and Extensions

10. **[Phase 10](#phase-10-png-export)**: PNG export
11. **[Phase 11](#phase-11-analogtimeseries-heatmap-variant-future)**: AnalogTimeSeries variant (separate widget)

### Open Questions

1. **Row label rendering**: Should unit labels be rendered as axis tick labels via `VerticalAxisWidget`,
   or as a separate label column? The axis approach reuses existing infrastructure but may not
   handle long labels well.

2. **Performance at scale**: What is the target unit count? 100 units × 200 bins = 20K rectangles
   is trivial for OpenGL. 1000 units × 1000 bins = 1M rectangles may need texture-based rendering
   instead of rectangle batches.

3. **AnalogTimeSeries variant timing**: Should the analog heatmap be a mode of this widget
   or a completely separate widget? The shared infrastructure is substantial, but the data
   pipelines are fundamentally different.

# See Also

- [HeatmapWidget Documentation](index.qmd) — Full architecture and feature documentation
- [Plot Widget Guide](../plot_widget_guide.md) — Shared architecture, axis types, DataManager integration
- [PSTHWidget](../PSTHWidget/index.qmd) — Single-unit PSTH; shares rate estimation pipeline
- [PSTHWidget Roadmap](../PSTHWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Rate estimation factoring, embeddable PSTH for tooltips
- [EventPlotWidget](../EventPlotWidget/index.qmd) — Trial-level raster plot; shares alignment and linking
- [EventPlotWidget Roadmap](../EventPlotWidget/roadmap.qmd) — Cross-widget linking, external feature coloring
- [SpectrogramWidget](../SpectrogramWidget/index.qmd) — Shares colormap infrastructure
